ab: nakamuan nu misu atu aku anucila namalahuk kapah tu haw?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: kapah tu, nakamuan nu mita icuwa masasuazih?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: nakamuan nu mita cacudadan kapah tu haw? amisasing kita.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: tayza pakayingan kapah tu, asilusid kaku tayza.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: niyazu’ay niyam a sakulal salungan ku bitelak. halhal tu bitelak nu balu katayni mikilim takuwan!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: halhal katukuh tu sadingsingan, mautang tu!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: katayni pipalamalan kisu!
ch: W: 那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: hang i! halhalen aku!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: hani kya malimad taluma’ i niyazu’ mauzip kisu?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: ataluma’ pasubana’ tu cudad kaku.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: mahica caay ka i tukay pasubana’ tu cudad?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: zayhan mabulah kaku tu niyazu’ aku, maydih aca pasukas tu niyazu’ay.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: matineng mitenun kisu, cima ku pasubana’ay tisuwan?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: u bai aku, u samatinengay mitenun a tademaw nu niyazu’ niyam ciniza.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: kya maydih minanam mitenun kisu?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: u lalangawan nu niyam ku mitenun, maydih a panayat tu lalangawan kaku.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: asu’ kanen kuyni a elaw.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: nu maku nisaelawan yu!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: mati:neng kisu, matenes misaelaw tuyni haw?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: caay, pakala tu cacay a lipay kapah tu. ayawen mihudhud mingaay ku belac atu hakhak, palamel han tu sapakulit papulung han mikiwakiw palalecad, pazeng han tu i labu nu tedus kapah tu.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
